title: Health Informatics Master Program Application and Fees
content:
Health Informatics Master Program Application and Fees

2025-2026 Spring Semester online application deadline: December 15, 2025

Application Deadline: Sunday, January 25, 2026

Results Announcement Date: Wednesday, January 28, 2026

Main List Registration Deadlines: January 28 - February 4, 2026

Reserve List Registration Deadlines: February 5 - 13, 2026

Click here

for Online Pre-Registration Link.

ALES Score Type:

Equal Weight (EA)

There will be no science exam;

1) In the program with thesis, 65% of ALES in Equal Weight score type, 35% of undergraduate graduation grade point average,

2) In the non-thesis program, 50% of the undergraduate graduation grade and 50% of the relevant score type ALES, if any*, will be evaluated.

*(If the candidates applying to non-thesis programs do not have ALES scores, they will be evaluated as 55 points.)

The applications of the candidates who do not apply online, do not graduate during the application, and do not upload their ALES document and their identity information to the application system will be deemed invalid.

Student Application Requirements to the Program

Candidates must have a bachelor's degree in order to apply for the master's program.

The equivalence of undergraduate diplomas obtained from abroad must be approved by the Council of Higher Education.

Applicants to the program with thesis must have scored at least 55* in Equal Weight from the Academic Personnel and Graduate Education Entrance Examination (ALES). Candidates who score at least equivalent to ALES in the internationally accepted "Graduate Record Examination" (GRE) or Graduate Management Admission Test (GMAT) exam, which are considered equivalent to the LES exam by the Interuniversity Board, are also evaluated. In the admission of students to graduate programs; ALES score, undergraduate grade point average and interview result are evaluated.

Documents Required During Final Registration

- Original or certified copy of the undergraduate diploma

-Original or certified copy of the undergraduate transcript (transcript)

-Photocopy of Identity Card

-Photo (2 Pieces)

-ALES result document for thesis program applications;

At least 55 points in the Equal Weight score type

-Applicants will be selected in order of success.
